title: write_xaiger2: Fix output port mapping when opaque boxes are present
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

While working on adding inout / undirected connection support to bufnorm and trying to make sure those survive `abc_new` I ran into sporadic bugs where unrelated wires would end up getting connected. Eventually I was able to track it down to inconsistent numbering of aiger output ports between the actual logic in the aiger file and the generated port mapping file.

_Explain how this is achieved._

The changed code looked like an easy to make bug and making this change fixed it. I don't really understand the reasoning behind the way the aiger2 backend allocates ports, so I'm not sure this change is generally correct, but the observed inconsistencies disappeared.

_If applicable, please suggest to reviewers how they can test the change._

I'm primarily looking for confirmation from @povik that this change is correct with respect to how this backend is designed. This should end up being exercised by a followup PR for the updated bufnorm handling.
